name: Fitness Reality 810XLT Super Max Power Cage

specs.Optional Add-Ons: Lat pulldown attachment, dip handles (sold separately)

whatWorks: 800 lb weight capacity is honest and handles serious powerlifting loads — owners report squatting 400+ lbs in this cage without any flex or instability in the uprights.

19 adjustable height positions for both J-hooks and safety bars cover the full range of barbell heights needed for squats, bench press, overhead press, and rack pulls — more positions than most competitors at this price.

J-hooks and safety bars together enable solo training without a spotter — the safety bars catch the bar if you fail a lift, which is the core safety function of a power cage and the reason to choose one over a squat stand.

The multi-grip pull-up bar (wide, narrow, and neutral grip positions) adds meaningful upper body training options beyond barbell movements, extending the utility of the cage without requiring additional equipment.
worthKnowing: Weld quality is inconsistent across production batches — some owners receive cages with rough welds or minor misalignments that require inspection and occasional touch-up. Inspect all welds on arrival and report issues immediately.
Assembly takes 3–4 hours solo due to the number of bolts and the need to align upright holes while holding the frame. Two people makes it a 1.5–2 hour job. Budget the time accordingly.
Bolts loosen with heavy use and should be checked and re-tightened every 2–3 months. Loctite thread compound on non-safety-critical bolts reduces re-tightening frequency.

Floor anchoring is strongly recommended for heavy squatting (300+ lbs) — without anchors the cage can tip slightly on eccentric loading. The hardware for anchoring is included but requires drilling into the floor.

commonComplaints.title: Weld quality inconsistent…
commonComplaints.body: Mine had a rough weld on one upright that I had to grind down before it was smooth. Not a structural issue but annoying on a safety piece of equipment.

commonComplaints.title: Assembly takes forever solo…
commonComplaints.body: 4 hours by myself. If I'd had help it would have been 2. The uprights need to be held while you bolt the crossbeams and you simply cannot do it alone efficiently.

commonComplaints.title: Bolts need regular tightening…
commonComplaints.body: I check all the bolts monthly. A few always need a turn or two. Not a big deal but something to be aware of for safety.

commonComplaints.title: Needs floor anchors for heavy use…
commonComplaints.body: I noticed the cage move slightly during a 350 lb squat. I anchored it the next day with lag bolts into the concrete and it's been rock solid since. Don't skip anchoring.

verdict: The Fitness Reality 810XLT Power Cage delivers genuine power cage functionality — 800 lb capacity, 19 height positions, safety bars, and a multi-grip pull-up bar — at a price point well below premium alternatives. The weld quality inspection and periodic bolt tightening are real maintenance responsibilities, not optional, and floor anchoring for heavy loads is essential rather than advisory. For home gym builders who want a safe solo training environment without spending $800–1,500 on premium alternatives, this cage punches above its price class.

realBuyers.quote: I've squatted 405 lbs in this cage and it didn't move. Solid piece of equipment for the price.
The safety bars gave me confidence to push my bench press harder than I ever could at home before. That peace of mind is worth the price alone.
Built my whole home gym around this cage. Pull-ups, squats, bench, overhead press — I do everything in here.
I pay nothing in gym fees anymore. This cage paid for itself in 6 months. Build quality is solid for what you're paying.

metaDescription: Fitness Reality 810XLT Power Cage review for Canada: 800 lb capacity, 19 height positions, multi-grip pull-up bar. Honest on weld quality and anchoring requirements. ~$449 CAD.
introText: The Fitness Reality 810XLT Super Max Power Cage is a 12-gauge steel power rack with 800 lb weight capacity, 19 adjustable height positions, and a multi-grip pull-up bar — the practical foundation for a serious home gym at a budget price. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B00MS20SHC) at around $449 CAD.
